title:
Module 4
SFW Video Creation
subtitle: Learn Motion Control with Kling and How to Generate Video Prompts

sections.header: Lesson 1: Introduction to AI Video with Kling

blocks.content: Video content dramatically increases engagement and reach on every platform. Kling is an AI video generation tool that takes your static images and turns them into realistic video clips with motion, expressions, and camera movement. This is how you bring your AI model to life.

items: Instagram Reels and TikTok prioritize video in discovery
Video makes your AI persona feel real and alive
Moving content stops the scroll far more than static images
Even short 3-5 second clips massively boost engagement
Video content converts followers to subscribers faster

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Screenshot of Kling interface / dashboard overview

items: Turn a single image into a short video clip
Add natural motion: hair movement, blinking, head turns
Control camera movement: pan, zoom, tracking
Generate lip sync and facial expressions
Produce videos in multiple aspect ratios
Instagram Reels (primary use case)
TikTok posts
Instagram Stories for 'casual' feel
Fanvue content to increase perceived value

sections.header: Lesson 2: Writing Video Prompts for Kling

blocks.content: Kling video generation is prompt-driven, just like image generation. But video prompts require you to think about motion, timing, and camera behavior — not just static appearance. A good video prompt describes what happens, not just what the scene looks like.

items: Start with the source image (your AI model image)
Describe the motion: what moves and how
Describe camera behavior: static, slow pan, zoom in
Describe the mood: calm, energetic, playful, intimate
Keep it simple — less motion = more realistic output

left.content: Prompt: 'Slow zoom in on woman's face, she looks at camera and gives a soft smile, slight head tilt to the right, wind gently moves her hair, natural daylight'

Prompt: 'Girl dancing and spinning around while the camera flies in circles'
(Too much motion — will produce distorted, unrealistic output)

items: One motion at a time produces the cleanest results
Subtle movements look more realistic than dramatic ones
Head turns, hair movement, and blinking are the easiest wins
Camera zoom-in works better than camera panning
Avoid full body movement — it's the hardest to get right

sections.header: Lesson 3: Exporting & Using Videos on Social Media

blocks.content: Generating a video is only half the job. You need to export it in the right format, at the right resolution, and post it with the right strategy for each platform. This lesson covers the final steps from Kling output to published content.

items: Resolution: 1080x1920 for Reels/TikTok (9:16 vertical)
Resolution: 1080x1080 for feed posts (1:1 square)
Format: MP4 is universal
Duration: 3-15 seconds is ideal for most platforms
Check for artifacts or glitches before exporting

left.title: IMAGE PLACEHOLDER
left.content: Export settings screenshot and format comparison chart

items: Instagram Reels — Add trending audio, hashtags, and a hook caption
TikTok — Similar to Reels, keep it native-feeling
Instagram Stories — Use raw-feeling clips for authenticity
Fanvue — Longer or more intimate clips for subscriber content
Face matches your base model consistently
No weird hand/finger movements
Motion looks natural, not robotic
No visual glitches or frame jumps
Audio (if added) syncs properly

blocks.title: Pro Tip
blocks.content: Start simple. A 5-second clip of your AI model looking at the camera with a subtle smile and a slow zoom-in will outperform a complex, glitchy 15-second video every time. Master the basics before trying advanced motion.

summary_points: Video content dramatically increases engagement and reach on all platforms
Kling turns your static AI images into realistic motion clips
Keep video prompts simple — one motion at a time produces the best results
Subtle movements (smile, head tilt, hair movement) are more realistic than dramatic ones
Export in 9:16 for Reels/TikTok, 1:1 for feed posts, always MP4
Quality check every video before posting — face consistency and no glitches
